options.text: Heavy infestations may result in diarrhea sometimes associated with blood
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.
Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: The eggs have a barrel-like appearance with bipolar plugs
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: After ingestion the eggs hatch into larvae that migrate through the lung before maturing in the duodenum
options.feedback: Correct! Trichuris does not have a lung migration phase.

options.text: The eggs passed in stool are unembryonated, so they require a time to mature in the soil before they become infectious
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

question: In the 1920's, the Rockefeller Foundation funded a physician to go throughout the Pacific Islands to decrease the incidence of hookworm. Each of the following but one would help decrease the incidence of this disease. Which one would not make any change in incidence of Ancylostoma duodenale or Necator americanus?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This would help.

options.text: Washing hands after using the bathroom
options.feedback: Correct! Hookworm is not transmitted by the fecal-oral route.
Incorrect. This would help.

options.text: Treating those found to be infected
options.feedback: Incorrect. This would help.
Incorrect. This would help.

question: An elderly Tongan male was diagnosed with multiple myeloma (a form of cancer) and has been treated with chemotherapy agents for two months. He was then admitted to the hospital with new-onset asthma symptoms that became worse when he was treated with steroids. Many small larvae were found in his sputum and he was found to have a hyper-infection with Strongyloides stercoralis. Which statement is false regarding Strongyloides?

options.text: Strongyloides is a common cause of iron deficiency anemia just like hookworm
options.feedback: Correct! Strongyloides does not cause significant blood loss.

options.text: The initial infection occurs when larvae penetrate the skin from the soil
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Auto infection occurs when the ova hatch and mature in the intestinal tract and then penetrate the intestine to establish a persistent infection
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Eosinophilia is present during the migrating phase of the life cycle
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Recurrent bacterial infections can develop when bacteria are carried by the larvae into sterile areas of the body
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

question: A physician calls from an outlying hospital to report that his patient has passed a large, white, round worm with pointed ends that is about 8 inches long. He is concerned this could be Echinococcus but the only parasite that matches this description is Ascaris lumbricoides. Which of the following is false regarding Ascaris lumbricoides?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Large numbers of larvae passing through the lung can cause asthma type symptoms
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Large numbers of adult worms in the gut can cause obstruction
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: The fertilized unembryonated egg passed in the stool requires a period of time to embryonate before it becomes infectious
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Heavy infection has been associated with rectal prolapse
options.feedback: Correct! Rectal prolapse is associated with Trichuris, not Ascaris.

question: Humans are the definitive host for Diphyllobothrium latum or the fish tapeworm which competes for Vitamin B12 with its human host. Vitamin B12 deficiency can result in an anemia with production of very large red blood cells, called macrocytic anemia. All of the following would prevent the spread of this disease but one. Which one would not be a method of prevention?

options.text: Avoid swimming in fresh waters that are endemic for Diphyllobothrium latum
options.feedback: Correct! Swimming does not cause infection; ingestion of fish does.

options.text: Cook freshwater fish well before eating
options.feedback: Incorrect. This would help.

options.text: Freeze fish that is going to be consumed raw which will kill the parasite
options.feedback: Incorrect. This would help.

options.text: Provide sewage treatment plants that treat raw sewage before dumping it into fresh water
options.feedback: Incorrect. This would help.
Incorrect. This would help.

question: Taenia saginata and Taenia solium are similar in many ways, but Taenia solium is more dangerous. Which of the following makes T. solium more dangerous?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a morphological difference, not the reason for danger.

options.text: It has fewer uterine branches than T. saginata
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a diagnostic difference.

options.text: Man can be infected as an intermediate host by eating the eggs from T. solium
options.feedback: Correct! This can cause cysticercosis, which is dangerous.

options.text: The cyst or larvae form ingested from pork is more potent than the cyst from beef
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the primary reason.

options.text: It produces more eggs than T. saginata
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the primary reason.

question: A 22-year old Tibetan female is found to have a large multilocular cyst of the pelvis that is removed and found to be due to Echinococcus granulosus. This is most unusual since most cysts occur in the liver or lung. Which of the following is false regarding Echinococcus granulosus?

options.text: The diagnosis in humans can be made by looking for the eggs in human stool
options.feedback: Correct! Humans are not definitive hosts, so they do not shed eggs.
Incorrect. This is a true statement.
Incorrect. This is a true statement.
Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Humans contract disease from eating the eggs excreted in dog feces
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

question: A student returns from a student teaching assignment in Zimbabwe. On the way home she swam in Lake Malawi. She is found to have schistosomiasis. Four of the following answers are good advice on how to prevent schistosomiasis. Which one is bad advice?

options.text: In risk areas, don't swim in fresh water rivers or lakes
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is good advice.

options.text: Swimming in chlorinated pools is safe
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is good advice.

options.text: Swimming in ocean salt water is safe
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is good advice.

options.text: Towing off vigorously after contact with potentially contaminated water decreases your risk
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is good advice.

options.text: Wading in water up to your knees is safe for brief periods of time
options.feedback: Correct! This is bad advice as cercariae can penetrate intact skin.

question: A Chinese male has had right upper quadrant pain for the past 10 years. Workup for gallbladder disease with an ultrasound shows small linear organisms. O&P exam reveals the ovoid, flask shaped egg of Clonorchis sinensis. Which of the following statements is false regarding Clonorchis sinensis?

options.text: It is also called the oriental liver fluke
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.
Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: This infection can best be treated with surgical removal of the gallbladder
options.feedback: Correct! Treatment is with antihelminthics like praziquantel.

options.text: There are two intermediate hosts - the snail and the fish
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

options.text: Long-term infection has been associated with cholangiocarcinoma (cancer of the bile duct)
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a true statement.

question: The major groups of arthropod-borne viruses include

options.feedback: Correct! These are the major arbovirus families.
Incorrect. Rhabdoviruses are not a major arbovirus group.
Incorrect. Enteroviruses are not arboviruses.
Incorrect. Retroviruses are not arboviruses.

question: The major pathological features in Yellow Fever include

options.text: Midzonal hepatic necrosis and Councilman bodies
options.feedback: Correct! These are characteristic features.

options.text: Midzonal hepatic necrosis and Negri bodies
options.feedback: Incorrect. Negri bodies are associated with rabies.
Incorrect. This is not typical.

options.text: Midzonal hepatic necrosis and diffuse encephalitis
options.feedback: Incorrect. Encephalitis is not a major feature.

question: The pathogenesis of dengue hemorrhagic shock syndrome is distinguished from that of uncomplicated dengue by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Dengue does not cause excitotoxic injury.
Incorrect. Meningitis is not the distinguishing feature.
Correct! DHF/DSS is associated with antibody-dependent enhancement.
Incorrect. Dengue is not neurotropic.

question: Rabies reaches the central nervous system by

options.feedback: Incorrect. Rabies is not primarily hematogenous.
Correct! Rabies travels along nerves.
Incorrect. This is not the primary route.
Incorrect. Only B is correct.

question: Creutzfeldt- Jakob disease is caused by

options.feedback: Incorrect. JC virus causes progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.
Incorrect. This is a hantavirus.
Correct! CJD is a prion disease.
Incorrect. SV40 is a simian virus.

question: Rabies virus produces infection of

options.feedback: Incorrect. Rabies primarily infects neurons.
Incorrect. Rabies primarily infects neurons.
Correct! Rabies is a neurotropic virus.
Incorrect. Rabies does not primarily infect macrophages.

question: The most frequent cause of viral meningitis in the United States is

options.feedback: Correct! Enteroviruses are the most common cause.
Incorrect. Rhabdoviruses are not a common cause.
Incorrect. This is a less common cause.
Incorrect. This is a less common cause.

question: Common symptoms of hepatitis include:

options.feedback: Incorrect. Diarrhea is not a classic symptom.
Correct! These are classic symptoms.

options.text: Nausea, vomiting, right upper quadrant abdominal pain
options.feedback: Incorrect. While possible, these are not the most classic triad.

options.text: Clear urine, dark colored stools, anorexia
options.feedback: Incorrect. Urine is dark and stools are light in hepatitis.

question: Hepatitis A can be transmitted by:

options.text: Changing diapers on an infected baby
options.feedback: Correct! HAV is transmitted fecal-orally.

options.text: Having sex with someone who is infected
options.feedback: Incorrect. While possible, this is less common.
Incorrect. This is not a transmission route.

options.text: Needle stick injuries from infected patients
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is more common for HBV and HCV.

question: A 30 year old patient comes in with symptoms of hepatitis. The following serological results are obtained: Hepatitis A IgM antibody negative, Hepatitis A IgG antibody positive, Hepatitis B surface antigen positive, Hepatitis B surface antibody negative, Hepatitis C antibody negative. This set of results suggests that the patient has acute:

options.feedback: Incorrect. HAV IgM is negative, indicating past infection or immunity.
Correct! HBsAg positive indicates acute or chronic HBV infection.
Incorrect. HCV antibody is negative.
Incorrect. Only Hepatitis B is indicated.

question: A major characteristic of hepatitis C is:

options.text: It is transmitted by eating raw oysters
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is associated with hepatitis A.

options.text: It is highly likely to cause chronic ongoing infection
options.feedback: Correct! HCV has a high rate of chronicity.

options.text: Most patients have severe symptoms when first infected
options.feedback: Incorrect. Most acute HCV infections are asymptomatic.
Incorrect. HCV is an RNA virus.

question: HIV is the same as:

options.feedback: Correct! HIV was formerly called HTLV-III.
Incorrect. HTLV-II is a different virus.
Incorrect. HTLV-I is a different virus.
Incorrect. HTLV-III is correct.

question: The likelihood of HIV being transmitted from a pregnant woman to her infant in the absence of antiretroviral therapy is about:

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is too low.
Incorrect. This is too low.
Correct! The transmission rate is approximately 20-25%.
Incorrect. This is too high.

question: One of the characteristics of HIV infection is that:

options.text: The virus multiplies slowly with a half-life of weeks matching the slow rate of disease progression
options.feedback: Incorrect. HIV has a rapid turnover.

options.text: There is rapid dissemination of virus within the first few weeks of infection to all tissues
options.feedback: Correct! HIV rapidly disseminates.

options.text: In early infection the virus is most actively multiplying within the bloodstream
options.feedback: Incorrect. It also multiplies in lymphoid tissue.

options.text: There is little if any immune response to HIV infection, and that is why this infection is so overwhelming
options.feedback: Incorrect. There is a robust immune response, but the virus evades it.

question: Serologic conversion to a positive test for HIV infection occurs:

options.feedback: Incorrect. Seroconversion takes longer.
Correct! This is the typical window period.
Incorrect. This is longer than typical.
Incorrect. This is not typical.

question: One of the key differences between amantidine/rimantidine and the newer neuraminidase inhibitor influenza virus drugs is:

options.feedback: Incorrect. Allergy is not a key difference.

options.text: antimetabolic effect on host cells due to lack of specificity for viral enzymes
options.feedback: Incorrect. Amantadine targets a viral protein.

options.text: rate of development of drug resistance
options.feedback: Correct! Resistance to amantadine develops rapidly.
Incorrect. Cost is not the key difference.

question: Which of the following best characterizes antiviral chemotherapy in comparison to bacterial chemotherapy?

options.feedback: Correct! Viruses rely on host cell machinery.
Incorrect. While sometimes true, this is not the best comparison.

options.text: Toxicity a minor problem, multiple enzymatic targets
options.feedback: Incorrect. Toxicity is a major problem.

options.text: Many more drug options are available to treat viral infections
options.feedback: Incorrect. Fewer drugs are available.

question: HIV reverse transcriptase, a critical target in modern antiretroviral chemotherapy, performs which critical function for the virus:

options.text: Cleaves polyprotein precursor leading to functional assembly of viral core
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is the function of protease.

options.text: Cleaves sialic acid residue from glycoprotein permitting attachment and entry into cell
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is the function of neuraminidase.
Correct! Reverse transcriptase converts RNA to DNA.
Incorrect. This is a host immune response.

question: Foscarnet interacts with which of the following viral enzymes:

options.feedback: Correct! Foscarnet inhibits viral DNA polymerase.
Incorrect. Foscarnet does not inhibit protease.
Incorrect. Foscarnet does not inhibit this enzyme.
Incorrect. Foscarnet inhibits DNA polymerase.

question: 133. Enteroviruses are most closely related to which of the following viruses.

options.feedback: Incorrect. Herpes simplex is a DNA virus.
Incorrect. Hepatitis C is a flavivirus.
Correct! Hepatitis A is a picornavirus, like enteroviruses.
Incorrect. Rotavirus is a reovirus.
Incorrect. Rubella is a togavirus.

question: The following statements are clearly true about enteroviruses EXCEPT:

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.

options.text: They can cause a variety of diseases which imitate bacterial infection
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.

options.text: They are an important cause of meningitis
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.

options.text: They are an important cause of diarrhea
options.feedback: Correct! Enteroviruses are not a primary cause of diarrhea.

question: All of the following are syndromes associated with enteroviruses EXCEPT:

options.feedback: Incorrect. Enteroviruses can cause conjunctivitis.
Correct! Enteroviruses are not associated with CAD.
Incorrect. Enteroviruses can cause myocarditis.
Incorrect. Enteroviruses can cause pericarditis.
Incorrect. Enteroviruses can cause pleuritis.

question: All of the following are true statements regarding viruses EXCEPT:

options.text: They contain both RNA and DNA
options.feedback: Correct! Viruses contain either RNA or DNA, not both.

options.text: The nucleic acid may be single or double stranded
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.
Incorrect. This is true.

options.text: They reproduce using host cell energy
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.

options.text: The infectious particle is called a virion
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is true.

question: Influenza virus hemagglutinins play a significant role in which of the following phases of viral replication?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Uncoating is not mediated by hemagglutinin.
Incorrect. Assembly is not mediated by hemagglutinin.
Incorrect. Release is mediated by neuraminidase.
Correct! Hemagglutinin binds to sialic acid for entry.
Incorrect. mRNA production is not mediated by hemagglutinin.

question: The identification of bacteria by serologic tests is based on the presence of specific antigens. Which of the following bacterial components is least likely to contain useful antigens?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ribosomes can be antigenic.
Incorrect. Cell wall contains antigens.
Incorrect. Capsule contains antigens.
Correct! Flagella are not typically used for serologic identification compared to O, H, and K antigens.

question: Each of the following statements concerning the Gram stain is correct except:

options.text: Escherichia coli stains pink because it has a thin peptidoglycan layer
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Streptococcus pyogens stains blue because it has a thick peptidoglycan layer
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Mycoplasma pneumoniae is not visible in the Gram's stain because it does not have a cell wall
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Mycobacterium tuberculosis stains blue because it has a thick lipid layer
options.feedback: Correct! M. tuberculosis stains poorly due to lipids; it requires acid-fast stain.

question: An outbreak of sepsis caused by Staphylococcus aureus has occurred in the newborn nursery. You are called upon to investigate. According to your knowledge of the normal flora, what is the most likely source of the organism?

options.feedback: Correct! S. aureus commonly colonizes the anterior nares.
Incorrect. S. aureus is not a major colonizer of the colon.
Incorrect. S. aureus can colonize but is less likely.
Incorrect. S. aureus can colonize but is less common.

question: Each of the following organisms is an important cause of urinary tract infections except:

options.feedback: Incorrect. K. pneumoniae can cause UTIs.
Incorrect. E. coli is the most common cause.
Correct! B. fragilis is an anaerobe and is not a common UTI pathogen.
Incorrect. P. mirabilis can cause UTIs.

question: A 30 year old woman has non bloody diarrhea for the past 14 hours. Which one of the following organisms is least likely to cause this illness?

options.feedback: Correct! S. pyogenes causes pharyngitis and skin infections, not diarrhea.
Incorrect. C. difficile can cause diarrhea.
Incorrect. Shigella can cause diarrhea.
Incorrect. Salmonella can cause diarrhea.

question: Each of the following agents is a recognized cause of diarrhea except

options.feedback: Incorrect. C. perfringens can cause diarrhea.
Incorrect. V. cholerae causes diarrhea.
Correct! E. faecalis is not a typical cause of diarrhea.
Incorrect. E. coli can cause diarrhea.

question: Each of the following statements about the classification of streptococci is correct except

options.text: Pneumococci (Streptococcus pneumoniae) are alpha-hemolytic and can be serotyped on the basis of their polysaccharide capsule
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Enterococci are group D streptococci and can be classified by their ability to grow 6.5% sodium chloride
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Viridans streptococci are identified by Lancefield grouping, which is based on the C carbohydrate in the cell wall
options.feedback: Correct! Viridans streptococci are not Lancefield grouped.

options.text: Although pneumococci and the viridans streptococci are alpha-hemolytic, they can be differentiated by the bile solubility test and their susceptibility to optochin
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

question: Which of the following bacteria has the lowest 50% infective dose (ID50)?

options.feedback: Incorrect. C. jejuni has a high ID50.
Incorrect. S. typhi has a higher ID50.
Incorrect. V. cholerae has a high ID50.
Correct! Shigella has a very low ID50 (as few as 10-100 organisms).

question: Which of the following disease is best diagnosed by serologic means?

options.feedback: Incorrect. TB is diagnosed by culture and microscopy.
Incorrect. Gonorrhea is diagnosed by culture or NAAT.
Incorrect. Actinomycosis is diagnosed by culture and histology.
Correct! Q fever is often diagnosed by serology.

question: The cogulase test is used to differentiate

options.feedback: Incorrect. This test is specific to staphylococci.
Correct! S. aureus is coagulase positive.
Incorrect. This is a catalase test differentiation.
Incorrect. This is not a coagulase test.

question: Of the five senses, which one must microbiologists rely upon when studying microorganisms?

options.feedback: Correct! Microbiologists rely on sight.
Incorrect. Smell is less useful.
Incorrect. Hearing is less useful.
Incorrect. Touch is less useful.
Incorrect. Taste is not used.

question: Microbiologists must contend with unique problems when studying microorganisms. What is not one of these problems?

options.text: Pure cultures do not exist in nature.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a problem.

options.text: Groups of microbes must be separated from each other prior to study.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a problem.

options.text: Microbes must be grown under artificial conditions in the laboratory.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a problem.

options.text: Microbes are invisible and widely distributed.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a problem.

options.text: Contamination of a microbial culture is unlikely.
options.feedback: Correct! Contamination is likely, not unlikely.

question: Which of the 'Five Is' promotes multiplication of microbes over a period of hours, days, or weeks?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Inoculation is placing the sample.
Correct! Incubation allows growth.
Incorrect. Isolation is separating colonies.
Incorrect. Inspection is observing.
Incorrect. Identification is determining the organism.

question: Which of the following is an example of a clinical sample?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Soil is environmental.
Incorrect. Water is environmental.
Correct! Feces is a clinical sample.
Incorrect. Sewage is environmental.
Incorrect. Food is environmental.

question: What method of isolating bacterial colonies involves the use of a hockey stick?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Streak plate uses a loop.
Incorrect. This uses a loop.
Incorrect. Pour plate does not use a hockey stick.
Correct! A hockey stick (spreader) is used.
Incorrect. This is not a method.

question: Which method of obtaining isolated colonies is most commonly used for most applications?

options.feedback: Correct! The streak plate is the most common.
Incorrect. This is not the most common.
Incorrect. This is used but less common.
Incorrect. This is used but less common.
Incorrect. This is not a method.

question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding the use of animals in biology and medicine?

options.text: Animals are used in drug and vaccine research to prevent harmful effects on humans with untested materials.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Animal models can be used to study disease progression.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Animals are important sources of immune products.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: Animals are used to determine the toxicity of certain organisms.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is correct.

options.text: All bacteria require an animal host in order to be grown in the laboratory.
options.feedback: Correct! This is false.

question: For what purpose are semisolid media used?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Semisolid media do not isolate discrete colonies.
Incorrect. This is not the main purpose.
Incorrect. This is not the main purpose.

options.text: Determination of motility of a culture
options.feedback: Correct! Semisolid media are used to check for motility.

options.text: Determination of viscosity of a culture
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the main purpose.

question: Solid media provide a solid surface on which discrete colonies may develop. The inclusion of what component in media allows for the formation of this solid surface?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Methylene blue is a dye.
Correct! Agar is the solidifying agent.
Incorrect. Thioglycollate is a reducing agent.
Incorrect. Indole is a byproduct.
Incorrect. H2S is a byproduct.

question: What type of medium contains at least one component that is not pure and does not have an exact chemical formula?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Synthetic media are chemically defined.
Incorrect. Defined media have known composition.
Correct! Complex media contain undefined components like peptone.
Incorrect. This is a category, not specific to undefined components.
Incorrect. Differential media differentiate organisms.

question: Cultivation of fastidious microorganisms requires the use of what type of media?

options.feedback: Correct! Enriched media contain extra nutrients.
Incorrect. Enumeration media are for counting.
Incorrect. Selective media inhibit certain organisms.
Incorrect. General purpose media support many organisms.
Incorrect. Anaerobic growth refers to conditions, not media type.

question: When unwanted microorganisms of unknown origin appear in a culture of known identity, the culture is said to be what?

options.feedback: Incorrect. A pure culture has only one organism.
Incorrect. Axenic means pure.
Correct! Contamination means unwanted organisms.
Incorrect. A mixed culture has known multiple organisms.
Incorrect. Subculture is transferring.

question: Which of the following is defined as the ability to distinguish or separate two adjacent objects or points from one another in a microscopic specimen?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Image is the visual representation.
Correct! Resolving power is the ability to distinguish details.
Incorrect. Reflection is light bouncing off.
Incorrect. Illuminate means to light.
Incorrect. Refraction is bending of light.

question: How many magnifying lenses does a compound light microscope contain?

options.feedback: Incorrect.
Incorrect. It contains more than one.
Correct! It has an objective and an ocular lens.
Incorrect.
Incorrect.

question: Why must a drop of oil be placed between the tip of the oil immersion lens and the specimen on a glass slide?

options.text: The oil increases the degree of refraction.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Oil decreases refraction.

options.text: The oil helps to illuminate the specimen.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the main reason.

options.text: The oil increases the total power of magnification.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Oil does not increase magnification.

options.text: The oil decreases the numerical aperture.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Oil increases the numerical aperture.

options.text: The oil prevents light from bending as it passes through the specimen.
options.feedback: Correct! Oil reduces light refraction.

question: When a microscopic image is brightly illuminated, but is surrounded by a black field, what type of microscope is being used?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Phase-contrast shows different densities.
Incorrect. Fluorescence shows emitted light.
Incorrect. Bright-field has a bright background.
Correct! Dark-field shows bright organisms on a dark background.
Incorrect. Electron microscopy uses electron beams.

question: What type of microscopy allows for the visualization of internal components within live, unstained specimens?

options.feedback: Correct! Phase-contrast is used for live, unstained specimens.
Incorrect. Fluorescence often requires staining.
Incorrect. Bright-field requires contrast.
Incorrect. Dark-field shows outlines, not internal details.
Incorrect. Electron microscopy uses fixed specimens.

question: What type of microscopy is commonly used in the clinical setting to diagnose infections caused by specific organisms?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Phase-contrast is not typically used for diagnosis.
Correct! Fluorescence microscopy is used for specific antibody staining.
Incorrect. Bright-field is used for general morphology.
Incorrect. Dark-field is used for specific organisms like Treponema.
Incorrect. Electron microscopy is not routinely used clinically.

question: The type of microscopy in which the surface of a specimen is bombarded with electrons is known as what?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Bright-light is not a type of electron microscopy.
Incorrect. TEM transmits electrons through the specimen.
Correct! SEM scans the surface with electrons.
Incorrect. Phase contrast is a light microscopy technique.
Incorrect. Immunofluorescence uses fluorescent dyes.

question: Bacteria stain more readily when what type of stain is utilized?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is too general.
Incorrect. Anionic stains are acidic.
Incorrect. Acidic stains are repelled by bacteria.
Incorrect. Negative staining stains the background.
Correct! Basic dyes have a positive charge and bind to bacteria.

question: Negative staining is useful for observing the presence of what?

options.feedback: Correct! Negative staining reveals capsules.
Incorrect. Motility is observed in wet mounts.
Incorrect. Endospores require special staining.
Incorrect. Mycolic acid requires acid-fast staining.
Incorrect. Flagella require special staining.

question: Why would a differential stain be used to stain a microscopic specimen?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Simple stains are effective for basic morphology.

options.text: Because bacteria require the use of more than one dye to become visible.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Simple stains use one dye.

options.text: Because differential stains contain both a negative and a positive dye.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the definition.

options.text: Because differential stains contrast two cell types or cell structures.
options.feedback: Correct! Differential stains differentiate.

options.text: Because simple staining is a complex procedure, whereas differential staining is straightforward.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Simple staining is straightforward.

question: Which of the following stains is not a differential stain?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Gram stain is a differential stain.
Incorrect. Acid-fast stain is a differential stain.
Incorrect. Endospore stain is a differential stain.
Correct! Granule stain is not a differential stain.
Incorrect. Flagellar stain is a differential stain.

question: When performing the Gram stain, what type of specimen is used?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Live specimens are not used.
Correct! Heat fixation is used.
Incorrect. The specimen is stained.
Incorrect. Metal coating is for SEM.
Incorrect. Acid treatment is for acid-fast stain.

question: Both simple and differential stained specimens allow for the visualization of all but which of the following?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Shape is visible.

options.text: Presence or absence of certain structures
options.feedback: Incorrect. Some structures are visible.
Incorrect. Arrangement is visible.
Incorrect. Biochemical composition is not visible.
Correct! Motility cannot be seen in stained specimens.

question: A 70 year old woman with severe chronic lung disease presented in the OPD of CHUK University Teaching Hospital with fever, productive cough with purulent sputum and worsening hypoxemia. Sputum sample was collected and sent to microbiology lab. Lab reported plenty of Polymorphonuclear leukocytes with both intracellular and extracellular gram negative diplococci. This organism grows well on 5% Sheep Blood Agar and Chocolate Agar. Later butyrate esterase test was done and was found to be positive. What is the most likely organism to be involved in this illness?

options.feedback: Incorrect. H. influenzae does not grow on blood agar without factors.
Correct! M. catarrhalis is a gram-negative diplococcus that is butyrate esterase positive.
Incorrect. N. gonorrhoeae is not typical in pneumonia.
Incorrect. N. lactamica is not a typical respiratory pathogen.
Incorrect. N. meningitidis is a cause of meningitis.

question: A 32 year old woman who is 10 weeks pregnant presents to the Obstetrics clinic for prenatal care. She has a history of treatment for syphilis 7 years previously. The results of serologic tests for syphilis are as follows: Non treponemal test RPR: Nonreactive Treponemal test (TP-PA) :reactive. Which of the following statement is most correct?

options.text: The baby is at high risk for congenital syphilis
options.feedback: Incorrect. The nonreactive RPR indicates no active disease.

options.text: The mother needs a lumbar puncture and a VDRL test for her CSF for neurosyphilis
options.feedback: Incorrect. No indication of active disease.

options.text: The mother needs to be treated again for syphilis
options.feedback: Incorrect. Treatment is not indicated.

options.text: The mother's previous treatment for syphilis was effective
options.feedback: Correct! Treponemal tests remain positive, but non-treponemal tests become negative after successful treatment.

question: A 26 year old sexually active woman presents to the OPD with purulent vaginal discharge and dysuria. While history taking, she admitted that she had unprotected sexual intercourse with a new partner a week ago. Which of the following diagnostic tests will be the most sensitive method to find the etiologic agents involved in this case?

options.feedback: Incorrect. EIA is less sensitive than molecular methods.
Incorrect. Culture is less sensitive than molecular methods.
Incorrect. Gram stain has limited sensitivity.
Correct! NAATs are the most sensitive.
Incorrect. Serology is not useful for acute diagnosis.

question: A 20 year old woman who reports unprotected sex with a new partner 2 weeks previously develops fever and left lower quadrant abdominal pain with onset in association with her menstrual period. Neisseria gonorrhoeae is cultured from her endocervix. The diagnosis is gonococcal pelvic inflammatory disease. What is the common sequela of this infection?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cervical cancer is associated with HPV.
Correct! PID can cause tubal scarring and infertility.
Incorrect. Urethral stricture is a complication of gonorrhea in men.
Incorrect. Fibroids are not a sequela of PID.
Incorrect. This is not a common sequela.

question: A 40 year old man is suffering from multiple episodes of disseminated gonococcal infection since last few years. Cultures of his urethra and knee fluid yield Neisseria gonorrhoeae. What is the most likely cause for this infection?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the cause.

options.text: Absent of lymphocyte adenosine deaminase activity
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is associated with SCID.

options.text: Deficiency of a late- acting complement component C5, C6, C7 or C8
options.feedback: Correct! Complement deficiencies predispose to disseminated gonococcal infections.
Incorrect. This is associated with fungal infections.
Incorrect. This is associated with respiratory infections.

question: Infection with which of the following agents can result in a false positive nontreponemal (VDRL or RPR) test for syphilis?

options.feedback: Incorrect. B. burgdorferi can cause false positives.
Incorrect. EBV can cause false positives.
Incorrect. S. pyogenes can cause false positives.
Incorrect. VZV can cause false positives.
Correct! All listed can cause false positive RPR/VDRL.

question: False positive nontreponemal (VDRL or RPR) test for syphilis is a common diagnostic problem. In which of the following conditions you can expect false positive VDRL or RPR result?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Leprosy can cause false positives.
Incorrect. SLE can cause false positives.
Incorrect. Malaria can cause false positives.
Incorrect. Measles can cause false positives.
Correct! All can cause false positives.

question: A 22 year old woman presents with a 2 cm ulcer on her labia majora. The lesion is painless and has a raised border. The differential diagnosis of this lesion includes:

options.feedback: Incorrect. Adenovirus is not a typical cause of genital ulcers.
Incorrect. C. trachomatis is not a typical cause of genital ulcers.
Incorrect. N. gonorrhoeae does not cause ulcers.
Incorrect. HPV causes warts, not ulcers.
Correct! A painless ulcer with raised borders is a chancre, characteristic of primary syphilis.

question: After returning from community posting from a rural village of Nepal, a 20 year old medical student admitted to the hospital with chief complaints of fever (39oC) and headache. While history taking, the told that he had swimming in the pond while being in the village where cows are also taken for bathing. Based on the clinical examination, leptospirosis is suspected. Which of the following test will help to confirm diagnosis?

options.text: Culture of CSF on blood and chocolate Agar
options.feedback: Incorrect. Leptospira requires special media.

options.text: Culture of urine on Human diploid fibroblast cells
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the standard method.

options.text: Testing acute and convalescent phase sera for antileptospiral antibodies
options.feedback: Correct! Serology (MAT) is the diagnostic method.

options.text: Testing acute and convalescent phase sera using the RPR test
options.feedback: Incorrect. RPR is for syphilis.

options.text: Testing serum by dark-field examination for the presence of leptospires
options.feedback: Incorrect. Dark-field is not very sensitive.

question: Which of the following animals is the source of the Leptospira interrogans?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Cattle can be a source.
Incorrect. Dogs can be a source.
Incorrect. Mice can be a source.
Incorrect. Swine can be a source.
Correct! All are reservoirs for Leptospira.

question: A 42-year-old pt with AIDS presents to your office with chronic, intractable, debilitating diarrhea. He says it began not long after returning from a camping trip. options.feedback: Incorrect. Amebiasis is also a possibility, but the exposure history points to Cryptosporidium.

Incorrect. Malaria does not cause chronic diarrhea.
Incorrect. Leishmaniasis does not cause chronic diarrhea.

question: What is the primary cause of Amebiasis?

options.feedback: Incorrect. T. gondii causes toxoplasmosis.
Correct! E. histolytica causes amebiasis.

Incorrect. D. latum is a tapeworm.
Incorrect. E. histolytica is the correct answer.

question: A 24-year-old male pt presents to your office with a fever, head ache, and lethargy. He has recently returned from a medical mission, which placed him in many underserved African villages. Your physical exam confirms the fever and also reveals significant lymphadenopathy. His girlfriend states she has noticed increased irritability over the past few days. Given the symptoms and history, what is the most likely diagnosis?

options.feedback: Incorrect. American trypanosomiasis is Chagas disease.

Incorrect. Malaria does not typically cause personality changes.
Incorrect. Toxoplasmosis is less likely in an immunocompetent host.
Incorrect. Orchitis is inflammation of the testicles.

question: The process by which uninfected bugs are allowed to feed on a pts blood, then the gut contents are examined for parasites 4-6 weeks later is called

options.feedback: Correct! Xenodiagnosis is used to diagnose Chagas disease.
Incorrect. This is not a real term.
Incorrect. This is not a real term.
Incorrect. This is not a real term.
Incorrect. This is not a real term.

question: Which of the following is true of Malaria?

options.text: Old men and young children do not get Malaria.
options.feedback: Incorrect. Malaria affects all ages.

options.text: You get it from eating undercooked fish.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes Diphyllobothrium.

options.text: It is strongly linked to Amoeba Naegleri Fowleri.
options.feedback: Incorrect. There is no link.

options.text: Must seek CDC involvement in the diagnosis, treatment, and management of Malaria.
options.feedback: Correct! The CDC should be consulted for malaria management.

options.text: It is caused by swimming in warm, fresh water.
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes Naegleria fowleri.

question: A biology graduate student where cently visited a tropical region of Africa presents with new visual impairment and the sensation that something is moving in her eye. She tells you that she is concerned because she had been warned about eye disease transmitted by black flies. When in Africa, she was in a river area, and despite her best efforts she received a lot of black fly bites. She also has some sub-cataneous nodules. If her infection was acquired by black fly bite, what is the most likely causative agent?

options.feedback: Incorrect. A. braziliense causes cutaneous larva migrans.
Incorrect. D. medinensis is transmitted by water fleas.
Incorrect. L. loa is transmitted by deer flies and causes eye worm.
Correct! O. volvulus is transmitted by black flies and causes river blindness.
Incorrect. W. bancrofti is transmitted by mosquitoes and causes elephantiasis.

question: A woman who imports food from Mexico and spends several months per year in rural Mexico had to have a compound leg fracture pinned and set in Mexico and has returned 3 days later. She now has signs of acute appendicitis and is taken to surgery in Houston. When her appendix is removed, it is found to contain a light-colored, 20.5-cm long roundworm and bile-stained, knobby eggs consistent with Ascaris. How did she acquire this infection?

options.text: Ingestion of water containing filariorm larva
options.feedback: Incorrect. Ascaris is transmitted by eggs.

Incorrect. This describes Taenia.

options.text: Ingestion of Ascaris eggs from contaminated food
options.feedback: Correct! Ascaris is transmitted by fecal-oral route.

question: A patient whose major source of protein is smoked and cooked fish develops what appears to be pernicious anemia. What parasite is noted for causing a look- alike vitamin B- 12 anemia in certain genetically predisposed infected individuals?

Incorrect. D. caninum is a dog tapeworm.
Incorrect. T. solium causes cysticercosis.

question: Which of the following protozoans is free living and acquisition does not generally indicate fecal contamination?

options.feedback: Correct! Acanthamoeba is free-living.

question: What is the most likely reason for a chloroquine-treated case of Plasmodium vivax relapsing?

options.text: P. vivax has a significant level of chloroquine resistance
options.feedback: Incorrect. Chloroquine resistance is more common in P. falciparum.

options.text: P. vivax has a persistent erythrocytic stage
options.feedback: Incorrect. The persistent stage is in the liver.

options.text: P. vivax has a persistent exoerythrocytic stage (hypnozoite)
options.feedback: Correct! Hypnozoites are dormant liver stages.

options.text: Chloroquine is not one of the drugs of choice
options.feedback: Incorrect. Chloroquine is a drug of choice for P. vivax.

question: How is Leishmania donovani transmitted?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Anopheles transmits malaria.

Incorrect. This is not the route.

question: How is Schistosoma haematobium transmitted?

options.text: Ingestion of raw or under cooked snail, frog, or snake
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is not the route.

options.text: Invasion of filariform larvae from soil
options.feedback: Incorrect. This describes hookworm.
Incorrect. This is not the route.

options.text: Standing or swimming in contaminated water
options.feedback: Correct! Cercariae penetrate the skin in water.

question: An untreated AIDS patient (CD4+count of 180 cells/mm3) from southern California has developed progressively severe headache and mental confusion, along with ataxia and retinocoroiditis. question: Which of the following is the tape worm acquired from eating undercooked pork?

question: What roundworm is most likely to be transmitted by ingestion of food or water contaminated with feces?

question: What round worm is transmitted by filari-form larvae that are found in the soil and penetrate the skin?

Incorrect. Taenia is a tapeworm.
Incorrect. Toxocara is transmitted by egg ingestion.

question: How Clonorchis sinensis (Chinese liver fluke) is most likely transmitted to humans?

options.feedback: Correct! Clonorchis is transmitted by eating raw or undercooked fish.
Incorrect. This is not the route.
Incorrect. This is not the route.
Incorrect. This is for Taenia.
Incorrect. This transmits Loa loa.

question: A rural subsistence farmer from Brazil dies of heart failure. His autopsy shows a greatly enlarged heart. What was the vector for the most likely infectious agent that may have been responsible for his death?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Ixodes transmits Borrelia.

Incorrect. Sand flies transmit Leishmania.
Incorrect. Tsetse flies transmit Trypanosoma.

question: A patient whose cently returned from camping in Canada presents with malabsorption diarrhea. How does the most likely agent cause the diarrhea?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Giardia does not require co-infection.
Incorrect. This is typical of bacteria.

options.text: Tissue invasion leading to an inflammatory response and prostaglandin production
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is more typical of Entamoeba.

question: Which of the following protozoans is transmitted primarily by the motile trophozoite form?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Balantidium is transmitted by cysts.

question: Which of the following bacteria can grow in acidic pH?

options.feedback: Incorrect. V. cholerae prefers alkaline pH.

Incorrect. Salmonella prefers neutral pH.

question: Cell size and cell number can be measured using

Incorrect. This measures number only.
Incorrect. Coulter counter is correct.

question: Which of the following is used for the proper maintenance and preservation of pure cultures?

options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a method.

options.text: Preservation by overlaying cultures with mineral oil
options.feedback: Incorrect. This is a method.
Incorrect. This is a method.
Correct! All are methods of preservation.

question: In the process of freeze drying, a dense cell suspension is placed in small vials and is frozen at

options.feedback: Correct! This is the typical temperature for freeze drying.
Incorrect. This temperature is too warm.
Incorrect. This temperature is too warm.
Incorrect. This temperature is too warm.

question: The solidifying agent commonly used in preparation of media is/are

options.feedback: Incorrect. Agar is the most common.
Incorrect. Silica gel is used for some applications.
Correct! Both can be used as solidifying agents.
Incorrect. Both can be used.

question: Which of the following chemotherapeutic agents may affect the growth of Campylobacter jejuni?

options.feedback: Incorrect. Vancomycin is not used for Campylobacter.
